channel.title: InsurTech Intelligence
channel.description: Noticias globales de insurtech, actualizadas cada 6 horas.

item.title: Premiums Will Skyrocket by 2035, and Discounts Not Enough for Wind Mitigation, Studies Say
item.description: But relying on the incentive of premium discounts is not enough to prompt widespread adoption of mitigation measures, especially for low- and moderate-income households, according to a new study that examined more than 20 years of data from Florida’s Citizens Property Insurance Corp. And property insurers in Florida offer discounts of as much as 44% for some mitigation steps, such as window and door opening protection. Home fortifications, from roof tie-downs to window protections, have been recognized as key factors in reducing losses, claims and premiums in hurricane-prone states like Florida.

item.title: Medical Chronology Software Built for Insurance Defense
item.description: That’s the largest documented in-house claims legal deployment we cite, and it sets the bar for what defense-grade medical chronology software has to do at carrier scale. So when defense paralegals evaluate medical chronology software , the questions look different. Medical chronology software is the tool that takes that week-long manual job and compresses it into a working afternoon, without sacrificing the source-linking you’ll need on cross.

item.title: Vero Insurance New Zealand hires Chloe Donnelly as marine underwriter
item.description: To view the full article and get unlimited access to InsuranceAsia News's exclusive insight across 5,000+ articles

item.title: European fund manager Arrow Group to acquire Australian-headquartered W&I MGA Fusion
item.description: To view the full article and get unlimited access to InsuranceAsia News's exclusive insight across 5,000+ articles

item.title: Marsh’s Wincy Choi swaps London for Hong Kong for finpro role
item.description: To view the full article and get unlimited access to InsuranceAsia News's exclusive insight across 5,000+ articles

item.title: Exploring cross-sectoral interconnections in resolution planning
item.description: Understanding the cross-sectoral interconnections and how they play out in a crisis will be central to that review. They say resolution planning is one of the few fields where bank, insurer, and financial market infrastructure experts all agree on something: nobody wants to be the one who actually needs to use the plan. A cross-sectoral perspective is not optional; it needs to be integrated into our thinking.

item.title: ILS portfolios remain focused on resilience despite 2026 hurricane season forecasts: SCOR IP
item.description: In a new report, SCOR Investment Partners has said that while outlooks for the 2026 Atlantic hurricane season appear to be more positive compared to recent years, largely due to the emergence of strong El Niño conditions over the summer, portfolio construction remains focused on diversification and resilience, with a key objective of maintaining a high level of shock‑absorption capacity. Beginning with El Niño/La Niña, SCOR Investment Partners explains that current estimates suggest a transition from a weak El Niño in early summer to a strong to very strong event by August–October, coinciding with the peak of the hurricane season. In its report, that focuses on the implications that the Atlantic hurricane season could have for the ILS market, the firm outlined that this year’s season is expected to be “significantly quieter than normal”, with activity projected to well below both recent years and long-term averages.

item.title: Linda Langenberg appointed Head of Section Property Treaty Global Clients and Lloyd’s at Munich Re
item.description: Munich Re, one of the world’s leading reinsurance, primary insurance and risk solutions providers, has appointed Linda Langenberg as Head of Section Property Treaty Global Clients and Lloyd’s. Most recently, she served as Senior Underwriter before being appointed Head of Section Property Treaty Global Clients and Lloyd’s. In her new role, Langenberg will work with Munich Re’s Property Treaty Global Clients and Lloyd’s team during a dynamic period for the market.

item.title: Intact sees higher than expected cat and large losses of $416m for Q2’26
item.description: Intact Financial Corporation, a Canadian property and casualty insurer, has reported that catastrophe and large losses were elevated in the second quarter and settled $247 million above the firm’s expectations on a pre-tax basis and net of reinsurance. Intact has confirmed that it also saw an unusually high level of large losses in the second quarter. Intact stated, “These losses included a higher frequency of fire claims and other property losses across various geographies and risk segments.

item.title: Kieran Wilson joins ASR as Senior Energy Underwriter
item.description: Africa Specialty Risks (ASR), the developing-markets-focused re/insurance group, has announced the appointment of Kieran Wilson as Senior Energy Underwriter. Wilson will be reporting to Suzan Pardesi, the Head of Energy and Deputy Active Underwriter for Syndicate 2454, who commented: “Kieran is an experienced underwriter with strong technical energy underwriting expertise and solid broker relationships. In his new position, based in London, Wilson will focus on expanding and underwriting ASR’s energy portfolio, collaborating closely with brokers to develop the group’s oil, gas, and broader energy operations throughout Africa and other emerging markets.

item.title: Deere Settles Lawsuit by FTC, States Over Equipment Repair Restrictions
item.description: The settlement requires Deere to give farmers and independent providers access to the same equipment diagnostic and other repair resources available to authorized dealers for 10 years, and access to new repair resources once more than 50% of authorized dealers have access. According to the FTC, Deere illegally amassed monopoly power over repairs for its farm equipment, leading to service delays and higher prices as well as greater profits. Deere agreed on July 8 to settle a lawsuit by the Federal Trade Commission and five U.S.

item.title: UK Water Regulator Finds Severn Trent Breached Wastewater Duties
item.description: UK water regulator Ofwat found that Severn Trent Plc had “serious failings” in how it deals with wastewater and sewage as part of a broad investigation into practices of utilities in the sector. Ofwat didn’t impose a fine on Severn Trent, which had already identified problems and invested £98 million ($131 million) to address the issues before the regulator opened its case, according to a statement on Wednesday. “Our investigation found serious and unacceptable breaches by Severn Trent Water — that is not in question and the company accepts it,” Lynn Parker, senior director for enforcement at Ofwat, said in the statement.

item.title: Hormuz Ship Traffic Grinds to Near Halt After US, Iran Strikes
item.description: Traffic through the Strait of Hormuz came to a near standstill on Thursday, after the US struck Iran for a second straight day as a fragile truce between the two sides looked increasingly shaky. The slowdown comes after a spate of Iranian attacks on vessels that had prompted the US strikes , while President Donald Trump also said the ceasefire with Iran was over. Read More: Some War Insurers Advise Shipowners to Pause Hormuz Voyages After Attacks: Sources While liquefied natural gas tanker traffic through the strait remained at a standstill, two empty vessels have recently entered the Gulf of Oman and are heading toward the eastern entrance of Hormuz.

item.title: Why so quiet? Asia (re)insurance market remains committed to hydrogen projects despite relative silence
item.description: To view the full article and get unlimited access to InsuranceAsia News's exclusive insight across 5,000+ articles

item.title: Steadfast extends exclusivity period for US$5.4bn Amwins, Dragoneer bid
item.description: To view the full article and get unlimited access to InsuranceAsia News's exclusive insight across 5,000+ articles

item.title: Zinnia highlights annuity technology partnerships as market demand grows
item.description: Zinnia announced that it is working with life insurers to accelerate annuity product launches and modernize policy administration as carriers invest in technology to meet growing demand for annuities. Bottom Line: While Zinnia highlights its technology partnerships, one employee warns about the overuse of technology. The life and annuity technology company said its platform enables carriers to launch new annuity products in as little as three months, supports straight-through processing rates of more than 95% for new business, and offers same-day policy issuance.

item.title: Arch Insurance launches voluntary employee benefits products
item.description: Arch Insurance North America launched a new suite of voluntary group insurance products for employers, brokers, and agents, expanding its employee benefits offerings with accident medical expense reimbursement, hospital cash indemnity, and annual travel insurance. The new products are designed to supplement employer-sponsored benefits without requiring medical underwriting. The launch expands Arch Insurance’s Accident & Health portfolio, which also includes short-term disability, paid family and medical leave, business travel accident, and Medical GAP insurance.

item.title: Judge Approves $46.75M Payout for 23andMe Data Breach Victims
item.description: bankruptcy judge on Tuesday approved a $46.75 million settlement for victims of a 2023 data breach at the genetic testing company 23andMe, which exposed genetic and other personal information of an estimated 6.9 million customers. The settlement will be reduced by $14.29 million previously disbursed in connection with the breach, making the total additional payout $32.46 million. It cited the data breach, related litigation, and both increased competition and falling demand for genetic testing products as reasons for its Chapter 11 filing.

item.title: NYC Crews Stabilize Midtown High-Rise, Reopen Nearby Streets
item.description: New York City officials began reopening streets and allowing people back into nearby buildings on Wednesday after crews took steps to stabilize a compromised section of a Midtown Manhattan high-rise that had forced evacuations a day earlier. Authorities had also evacuated nearby office buildings and a school and closed streets between East 40th and East 45th streets from First Avenue to Third Avenue as engineers assessed the damage. The so-called frozen zone closed to vehicle and pedestrian traffic was reduced to 42nd and 43rd Streets between Second and Third Avenues, and five buildings in the area remain under full or partial evacuation orders.

item.title: Construction Firms Embrace Safety Programs to Reduce Serious Injury, Fatalities
item.description: Nearly two-thirds of construction firms have implemented a program to prevent serious injuries and fatalities, commonly called SIFs, according to a recent survey conducted by the Construction Safety Research Alliance (CSRA). For its annual Safety in Practice Report, researchers examined responses from 72 construction firms on seven safety concepts: Results indicate HECA adoption is increasing, with 26% of the firms implementing a program last year – up from 20% in 2024. Meanwhile, 63% of the firms don’t have safety metrics tied to management or employee incentives or bonuses, or metrics used to track regulatory compliance.

item.title: Cross-Border Payments: Towards the Next Chapter
item.description: The goal that has now become something of a mantra; it was “to make cross-border payments faster, cheaper, and more transparent and inclusive”. But cross-border payments are somewhat marginal to the business model of most of the companies which provide this service. We talk about global cross border payments, but its more complex than that phrase suggests.

item.title: FCA charges individual with insider dealing
item.description: Richard Bloomfield has been charged by the FCA with 5 counts of insider dealing. The FCA alleges that in his role as a solicitor at a law firm, Mr Bloomfield worked on an acquisition of Seraphine Group PLC and used inside information obtained through his role to deal in securities of Seraphine Group PLC on 5 occasions between 28 March 2022 and 10 January 2023. Mr Bloomfield appeared before Westminster Magistrates’ Court and gave no indication of plea.

item.title: Exclusive: EdVisorly Raises $13.3M Series A To Fix The Messy College Transfer Process With AI
item.description: EdVisorly aims to take the slow, manual paperwork out of the college admissions and transfer process. When Manny Smith was a high school senior, attending college didn’t look like an option. His parents had no college credits, the family couldn’t afford tuition, and he didn’t know how to apply.

item.title: Another massive data breach exposed millions of driver’s license numbers
item.description: insurance provider AssuranceAmerica has confirmed a data breach affecting the personal information and driver’s license numbers of 6.9 million people, making it the largest known spill of Americans’ driver’s license information this year. In June, the Texas state government said hackers stole information about at least 3 million driver’s licenses and passport numbers during a data breach affecting the state’s parks and wildlife division. The company concluded its investigation on June 15, finding that the hackers had stolen customers’ names, contact information, and driver’s license numbers.

item.title: TSR raises hurricane forecast slightly on potential for more favourable sub-tropical conditions
item.description: Tropical Storm Risk (TSR) has raised its forecast for the 2026 Atlantic hurricane season slightly, now calling for one more hurricane and major hurricane to form, citing “indications that the sub-tropical Atlantic may provide more favourable conditions for tropical cyclones.” At its April forecast update, Tropical Storm Risk (TSR) had lowered its previous long-range forecast to call for 12 named tropical storms, 5 hurricanes and just 1 major hurricane for the 2026 Atlantic hurricane season, with expected accumulated cyclone energy (ACE) of 66. The reason for raising the forecast is because there are indications that the sub-tropical Atlantic may provide more favourable conditions for tropical cyclones, which may slightly offset the largely unfavourable conditions anticipated across the tropical Atlantic and Caribbean Sea.” TSR’s forecast for US landfalling tropical storms for the 2026 hurricane season still remain unchanged, at 1 hurricane and 3 tropical storms to hit the coastline, below the 30-year norm of 1.6 and 3.8 respectively. However, the sub-tropical Atlantic is expected to have more favourable conditions, with higher sea surface temperature anomalies, above-average precipitation and lower vertical wind shear, which is why the forecast numbers were increased slightly, TSR explained.

item.title: CSU lowers 2026 Atlantic hurricane forecast further, citing El Niño and wind shear
item.description: The Colorado State University (CSU) tropical meteorology team has now further reduced its seasonal forecast for the 2026 Atlantic hurricane season , citing the high-likelihood of a strong El Niño and the associated increase in vertical wind shear as the primary factor. The CSU team explained, “We further reduce our 2026 Atlantic basin hurricane season forecast and now anticipate a well below-normal season. We anticipate the powerful El Niño being the dominant factor for the upcoming hurricane season, driving high levels of tropical Atlantic vertical wind shear.

item.title: QBE North America launches QCyber Rapid Pay to accelerate cyber recovery support
item.description: QBE North America, part of the global insurer QBE, has introduced QCyber Rapid Pay, a cyber insurance enhancement designed to accelerate financial support following a covered cyber event. To address this, QCyber Rapid Pay provides earlier access to liquidity, a more efficient claims process, and a faster path to recovery after an incident. By improving the timing and clarity of financial support, QCyber Rapid Pay helps deliver timely, predictable cash flow and a more streamlined claims experience.

item.title: Hyperexponential launches Hyperoperator to automate commercial P&C underwriting workflows
item.description: Hyperexponential (hx), a technology company that develops underwriting and pricing software for commercial property and casualty (P&C) insurers, has introduced Hyperoperator, an AI-powered underwriting agent designed to progress broker submissions from initial receipt to a decision-ready file while operating within an insurer’s existing pricing models, underwriting appetite and delegated authority framework. hyperoperator gives underwriters leverage without taking judgment out of their hands.” According to Hyperexponential, the platform is used by leading global insurers to support underwriting decisions representing more than $75 billion in annual commercial P&C premium, compared with $45 billion a year earlier. Hyperoperator, according to hyperexponential, has been developed to work within each insurer’s underwriting strategy, governance framework and decision-making processes rather than alongside them.

item.title: Gallagher Re reports 18% YoY rise in ceded reinsurance premium as US MGA market expands
item.description: Gallagher & Co., has reported that premium ceded by its carrier composite into the reinsurance market increased by 18% year-on-year during 2025, reflecting continued demand for reinsurance capacity from the expanding US managing general agent (MGA) sector. Gallagher Re says its carrier composite ceded $21.2 billion of premium into the reinsurance market during 2025, including significant placements with captive reinsurers. According to Gallagher Re, the five largest reinsurance counterparties assumed $4.8 billion of premium, equivalent to 22.7% of all ceded premium within the composite, while the ten largest accounted for 34%.

item.title: AM Best Revises Outlook to Stable for Brotherhood Mutual
item.description: AM Best has revised the outlook to stable from negative for the Long-Term Issuer Credit Rating (Long-Term ICR) and affirmed the Financial Strength Rating (FSR) of B++ (Good) and the Long-Term ICR of “bbb+” (Good) of Brotherhood Mutual Insurance Company (Brotherhood) (Fort Wayne, IN). The revision of the Long-Term ICR outlook to stable from negative reflects improved balance sheet strength fundamentals in recent years, supported by very strong risk-adjusted capitalization, as measured by Best’s Capital Adequacy Ratio (BCAR), driven by favorable underwriting results and strong investment income generated from its robust investment portfolio and dividends from affiliates and improved, although elevated, leverage measures. The Credit Ratings (ratings) reflect Brotherhood’s balance sheet strength, which AM Best assesses as strong, as well as its adequate operating performance, neutral business profile and appropriate enterprise risk management.

item.title: Some War Insurers Advise Shipowners to Pause Hormuz Voyages After Attacks: Sources
item.description: Some war underwriters have advised shipping companies to pause voyages through the Strait of Hormuz while others are reviewing their policy terms after renewed vessel attacks threatened a return to war between Iran and the U.S., insurance industry sources said on Wednesday. The U.N.’s International Maritime Organization (IMO) said on Wednesday that sailings through Hormuz should be avoided “as long as the safety and security of crews cannot be assured.” Speaking on Wednesday, IMO Secretary-General Arsenio Dominguez said the continued high cost of ship insurance in the region was a great concern, “compounding the strain on shipowners and operators.” Tuesday’s attacks on three tankers in the critical waterway prompted Washington to revoke a license allowing Iran to sell oil and launch strikes on Iranian targets overnight.

item.title: Q&A With NYC Mayor and Officials on Structurally Compromised Midtown Building
item.description: The following is an unedited transcript provided by New York City of a July 7, 2026 press conference with Mayor Zohran Kwame Mamdani, Fire Commissioner Lillian Bonsignore, Fire Department Chief John Esposito, and Buildings Commissioner Ahmed Tigani concerning the unstable building at a construction site on East 42nd Street. Mayor Zohran Kwame Mamdani: Good afternoon, everyone. This is an existing 37-story building with active permits to convert it from a commercial office building to a residential building.

item.title: Aviva and AXIS Launch First Lloyd’s Consortium Focused on UK Onshore Renewable Energy
item.description: The post Aviva and AXIS Launch First Lloyd’s Consortium Focused on UK Onshore Renewable Energy appeared first on Insurtech Insights . Aviva and AXIS have launched a new Lloyd’s consortium, the A-Squared Consortium, with £200 million of capacity to support UK onshore renewable energy projects and the country’s 2030 net zero ambitions. Aviva and AXIS have launched a new Lloyd’s consortium, the A-Squared Consortium, with £200 million of capacity to support UK onshore renewable energy projects and the country’s 2030 net zero ambitions.

item.title: NN Group Uses In-House AI to Settle Claims in Around Six Minutes
item.description: NN Group’s Dutch insurance business, Nationale-Nederlanden, is using AI to automate parts of its claims process, enabling some straightforward claims to be assessed and paid in around six minutes without manual intervention. NN Group’s Dutch insurance business, Nationale-Nederlanden, is using AI to automate parts of its claims process, enabling some straightforward claims to be assessed and paid in around six minutes without manual intervention. By building the solution in-house and retaining a customer route to manual review, NN Group signals an approach that pairs speed and efficiency with oversight, an emphasis regulators and customers increasingly expect as AI is applied to claims decisions.

item.title: PGI Canada launches personal guarantee insurance for business loans
item.description: PGI Canada has launched PGicover, a personal guarantee insurance product for Canadian business owners entering into commercial loan agreements. PGicover is designed to protect business owners from a portion of the personal financial liability they assume when signing personal guarantees, which are commonly required for business loans. The launch builds on Purbeck and Markel’s partnership in the UK, where they have offered personal guarantee insurance since 2017.

item.title: Stand and Moen launch smart water leak program with insurance discounts
item.description: Stand Insurance Solutions and Moen have launched a program that links Moen’s Flo smart water shutoff device with homeowners insurance discounts, giving policyholders financial incentives to reduce the risk of water damage. The Moen Flo system monitors water pressure and flow and can automatically shut off a home’s main water supply when it detects a catastrophic leak or burst pipe. As part of Stand’s Florida launch, the first 500 policyholders who enroll will receive a $49 credit toward the subscription activation fee in addition to annual insurance premium savings.

item.title: Honda pauses insurance agency operations amid market uncertainty
item.description: American Honda Finance Corporation (AHFC) has paused operations of American Honda Insurance Solutions (AHIS), less than a year after launching the licensed insurance agency. Honda introduced the agency in 2025 to offer Honda and Acura customers access to auto, motorcycle, RV, homeowners, and other insurance products through a partnership with VIU by HUB . In a statement to Coverager, AHFC said: “American Honda Finance Corporation (AHFC) has paused operations of American Honda Insurance Solutions (AHIS).

item.title: Allianz Unit to Cut as Many as 1,800 Jobs in Push to Adopt AI
item.description: Related: AI’s Impact: Tech and Finance Sectors Losing 28,000 Jobs Monthly Allianz Partners, which employs more than 22,000 people, has previously warned that staffing levels could be impacted by AI-driven transformations. Related: OpenAI’s Altman Says AI Unlikely to Lead to ‘Jobs Apocalypse’ Munich Re’s primary insurance unit Ergo aims to cut about 1,000 positions in Germany partly as a result of its increased use of artificial intelligence, Bloomberg has reported. Allianz SE plans to eliminate hundreds of positions at its assistance and travel insurance subsidiary Allianz Partners as the company pushes forward with implementing artificial intelligence solutions.

item.title: RGA inks block life deals with Sumitomo Life, Anshin Life
item.description: To view the full article and get unlimited access to InsuranceAsia News's exclusive insight across 5,000+ articles

item.title: Supply shocks to slow global premium growth as fragmentation reshapes risk landscape: Swiss Re
item.description: To view the full article and get unlimited access to InsuranceAsia News's exclusive insight across 5,000+ articles

item.title: FCA appoints new members to the Secondary Markets Advisory Committee
item.description: The FCA has appointed members to its advisory committee on secondary markets for the period July 2026 to July 2028. It supports the work of the FCA by: The Secondary Markets Advisory Committee members are: The committee will be chaired by Jon Relleen (Director of Infrastructure & Exchanges) and the Trading Policy team provides secretariat support. The Secondary Markets Advisory Committee supports the FCA’s work in wholesale secondary markets in equities, fixed income, foreign exchange, and commodities markets across securities, futures, swaps, and options markets.

item.title: Showing our workings: solving for growth, risk and trust
item.description: I was struck by how differently teachers approach problem-solving now: with creativity. Because the problems in front of us are big, including the UK’s productivity and growth challenge. Speech by Sarah Pritchard, deputy chief executive, at a Breakfast Briefing at The Whitehall Industry Group.

item.title: Your SaaS Metrics Are A Result, Not A Strategy
item.description: They are the result of strategy — or sometimes the result of a lack of it. LTV/CAC is one of the most important SaaS metrics. But they do not shine a light on strategy.

item.title: Familiar Names Top Active US Investor Ranks In Q2
item.description: All are names that repeatedly show up high in active investor rankings. Below, we ranked the 19 most active investors for Q2: When we narrow the ranks to only lead investors in venture rounds, the lineup changes some, but not dramatically. Per Crunchbase data, General Catalyst and Andreessen Horowitz stood out among the most active U.S.

item.title: Depth of non-life soft market downturn to be more limited this time: Swiss Re
item.description: Swiss Re has said that while the non-life insurance and reinsurance market has entered a softer phase of the underwriting cycle, it does not anticipate the depth of the downturn being as significant as we’ve seen in the past. On prospects for the global re/insurance market Swiss Re explains that, “Global non-life real premium growth is expected to soften to 0.6% in 2026, reflecting competitive pricing conditions and moderating economic momentum, while life insurance growth will stay robust at 2.3%, supported by higher yields.” At the same time, development of artificial intelligence (AI) infrastructure and the afore-mentioned geopolitical fragmentation, are driving new demand for resilience and risk transfer solutions, Swiss Re said. These interconnected exposures call for solutions that go beyond traditional insurance, combining risk engineering, alternative risk transfer and financing to help businesses invest with greater resilience.” On non-life insurance premium, Swiss Re notes this sector is “entering a softer phase of the underwriting cycle.” But the reinsurance company noted that, “this cycle is unfolding in a different environment from previous ones.” Leading Swiss Re to suggest that, “Rising claims inflation, geopolitical uncertainty and growing catastrophe exposures are likely to limit the depth of the downturn.”

item.title: Oxbridge Re closes five tokenized reinsurance security offerings, raising $7.1m on Solana
item.description: Oxbridge Re Holdings Ltd., the Cayman Islands-based reinsurance company, along with its subsidiary SurancePlus, has successfully closed five private placements of tokenized reinsurance securities on Solana blockchain, with total gross proceeds of combined offerings amounting to approximately $7.1 million. The tokenized offerings include three tokenized reinsurance securities providing qualified investors with synthetic contractual exposure to designated reinsurance risk associated with HCI Group, Inc.’s Fortex Reinsurance SPC, Ltd., together with Oxbridge Re’s traditional tokenized reinsurance offerings. We reported last month that HCI Group was tapping into the wave of interest in taking risk on-chain and issuing tokenized reinsurance securities as a way to access risk capital through a partnership with Oxbridge Re.

item.title: Ambassador mutual cat bond and ILW fund nears $900m in assets
item.description: When we last reported on Embassy’s Ambassador mutual cat bond and ILS fund strategy back in January , it had grown its total net assets under management to a new high of just over US $700 million, while the strategy delivered an 11.61% return for the last year of record. The Ambassador US mutual fund strategy operated by Embassy Asset Management that allocates to catastrophe bonds and private insurance-linked securities (ILS) that we believe to be largely ILW’s, has grown its assets under management further to reach almost $900 million as of this week. The Ambassador Fund has been growing steadily for Embassy Asset Management, further demonstrating the attraction mutual fund investors have to the catastrophe bond and related ILS investments.

item.title: RGA expands Japan reinsurance activity through two coinsurance agreements
item.description: RGA, a global life and health reinsurance company supporting insurers with risk management and financial solutions, has completed two block coinsurance agreements in Japan with Sumitomo Life Insurance Company and Tokio Marine & Nichido Life Insurance Co., Ltd. RGA said the agreements demonstrate the increasing role of coinsurance arrangements in Japan, as insurers explore additional options for managing in-force portfolios, improving capital efficiency and responding to evolving business requirements. (Anshin Life) involving the reinsurance of a block of whole life policies through a coinsurance structure.

item.title: Aon reveals new leadership structure for Reinsurance in UK and Ireland
item.description: This new leadership structure strengthens our ability to bring the best of Aon to clients across the UK and Ireland, enhancing our focus on growth, innovation and operational excellence.” Large, global insurance and reinsurance broking group Aon has expanded Tom Murray’s role to Head of Reinsurance for the UK and Ireland, and appointed new leaders within the team to support the efficient delivery of solutions and services to clients. Effective immediately, Aon has appointed Andrew Buckland as Chief Operating Officer of the Reinsurance UK and Ireland team.

item.title: Acrisure launches new service for UK high-net-worth individuals and families
item.description: Acrisure, a global fintech company offering insurance, reinsurance, real estate and financial services, has launched Acrisure Private Office, designed to support high-net-worth individuals and families in the UK dealing with complex personal insurance needs. This initiative follows Acrisure’s acquisition of Smith Greenfield earlier this year, integrating specialised private client expertise into the company. By offering a single, trusted relationship to manage and coordinate all insurance requirements, Acrisure Private Office provides clients with the tailored care of a boutique adviser alongside the extensive backing of a global enterprise.
